subject: Re: errors during ts_etomo_patches and ts_reconstruct
body:
Hi Reika,
glibc 2.17 was released in 2012, almost 15 years ago… sorry but we cannot support platforms like this, you would have to find your own solution in this case
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Jul 24, 2025, at 14:43, Reika Watanabe <reika.watanabe.c@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿
Dear Alister and others,
I am using the newest version of warp with CentOS 7.9.
# Name                    Version                   Build  Channel
warp                      2.0.0dev34              py311_0    warpem
LSB Version:
 
:core-4.1-amd64:core-4.1-ia32:core-4.1-noarch
Distributor ID:
 
CentOS
Description:
 
CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)
Release:
 
7.9.2009
Codename:
 
Core
I cannot run ts_etomo_patches. (error message attached below)
Since I could not run ts_etomo_patches, I did ts_stack. I  got the error message (error message below) but at least I can find the tiltstack folder created and I can find aligned tilt. After manual alignment with Etomo, I imported alignment,  and  I tried to run ts_reconstruct. I again got the error message and reconstruction folder is empty.
After investigating error message with ChatGPT, it seems that my glibc version is too old. 2.17.
What do you recommend ?
Are there any way to avoid making png?
WarpTools used to work well without error with this linux machine and I am using the same data set it used to work.
Thank you very much for your kind attention in advance.
 With best wishes,
Reika
